title: Abort/Fatal Python error from calling `AtomicDict()._rehash()` with unhashable object
author: devdanzin
body:
Running the following code results in a Fatal Python error:
```python
import cereggii

cereggii.AtomicDict()._rehash(set())
```

Error message:
```
Fatal Python error: _Py_CheckFunctionResult: a function returned a result with an exception set
Python runtime state: initialized
TypeError: unhashable type: 'set'

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

SystemError: <method '_rehash' of 'cereggii.AtomicDict' objects> returned a result with an exception set

Stack (most recent call first):
  File "/mnt/c/Users/ddini/crashers/cereggii/crash_04.py", line 3 in <module>
Aborted (core dumped)
```

As we discussed, this one isn't super important because it's for internal testing. Filing now because I forgot to.
